blockingIssues.description: Directory-scoped `REVIEW.md` is still collapsed into one run-wide `TrustedRules.ReviewMarkdown` winner rather than applying the nearest directory review per changed file/path.
`.gitlab/ai-review.yaml` loader behavior still suppresses unknown-field and validation warnings because parser warnings are dropped and unknown keys are silently accepted.

`/ai-review rerun` and `/ai-review focus` generate idempotency keys with `time.Now().UnixNano()`, so command retries/replays can create duplicate runs instead of at-most-once command effects keyed to the note delivery.

Note hook persistence and command execution are not atomic; if command execution fails after the hook event insert, a retried delivery is deduplicated and the rerun/ignore/resolve/focus side effect is permanently lost.

Degradation summary rendering now depends on `review_runs.error_detail`, but the degradation path never persists that summary there, so degraded runs fall back to a generic summary instead of the required explicit partial-coverage note.
The degradation branch does not persist the skipped-file/why summary where the writer can read it, so VAL-BETA-018 is not satisfied end-to-end.
The requested VAL-PERF-001 benchmark corpus/report is still missing from the implementation and worker handoff.
The requested VAL-PERF-001 benchmark corpus, runnable harness, and checked-in runtime evidence are still missing from the repository and mission handoffs.
Runtime provider construction in `cmd/worker/main.go` is still hard-coded to `default`/`secondary` route names and never consumes effective project/group `ProviderRoute` policy end-to-end.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how workers should handle features whose intended behavior is already present in dirty branch state or prior commits: require them to distinguish between true implementation work and tree-state finalization/validation-unblock commits, and to verify end-to-end runtime behavior rather than only metadata/tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `beta-command-webhooks-idempotency-atomicity-fix`, `beta-provider-route-runtime-wiring-fix`, and `beta-degradation-summary-persistence-fix` all lacked a concrete fix session or end-to-end proof, forcing reviewers to conclude against the still-current original implementation rather than a dedicated corrective change.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify rerun/fix-review procedure when a follow-up fix feature has no completed worker session, transcript, handoff, or commit: reviewers should explicitly record that absence and treat the original implementation as still current instead of assuming a fix exists.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `beta-command-webhooks-idempotency-atomicity-fix`, `beta-degradation-summary-persistence-fix`, `beta-large-mr-benchmark-evidence`, and `beta-provider-route-runtime-wiring-fix` all had null `completedWorkerSessionId` values, leaving reviewers to infer from mission state and current repo history that no fix artifact had shipped.

rejectedObservations.observation: The library already records that trusted REVIEW instructions are modeled as one run-wide string today, which is incompatible with beta's per-path directory REVIEW behavior.
